task: Heuristic Lab v3 - READ-ONLY eval.py leaf-term audit (section 11; no weights changed)

W_BODY.represents: Pokemon count (a KO costs a body)

W_ENERGY.status: PRESENT but deck-agnostic -- counts energy on ANY active, incl. Cinderace; does NOT distinguish a real attacker

W_V3_BACKUP_ATTACKER.status: OFF + Alakazam-specific -> no Starmie backup term

W_CA_HAND.status: PRESENT in evaluate_ca but NOT used by evaluate_deck_v3 (the deckout leaf the agent uses)

KEY_FINDING: The search leaf is DECK-AGNOSTIC and cannot tell a Mega Starmie ex attacker (Jetting 120 / Nebula 210) from Cinderace (Turbo Flare 50). W_ENERGY rewards energy on ANY active equally, so search has no reason to prefer promoting Mega Starmie over looping Cinderace. THIS is the search-level root of the Turbo-Flare-loop / 0-prize losses -- the heuristics keep patching what the leaf eval is blind to.

proposed_terms_ranked.note: value having a Mega Starmie ex active/promotable that can pay Jetting (1 W) or Nebula (3); de-value an active Cinderace that can only Turbo Flare. Would make search prefer promoting the real attacker.
penalize exposing our Mega Starmie ex (KO on it = opp takes 3 prizes); currently W_BODY treats it like any body.
a benched energized Mega Starmie ready to promote -> avoids the collapse when the active Mega dies.
reward energy concentrated on the Mega Starmie line, not spread or stuck on Cinderace.
whether the opponent active can KO our active next turn; needs opponent damage estimate (attack_stats unreliable) -> opponent-model territory, not a naive leaf term.

recommendation: Do NOT change weights in this task. The highest-leverage follow-up is a Starmie-aware leaf term for ATTACK READINESS (value a promotable Mega Starmie attacker over a Turbo-Flaring Cinderace) -- it addresses the diagnosed root, not just the symptoms. Validate any leaf change with isolated tests + a field A/B, separately from the three rule candidates.
